worksheets.title: Revenue Model Planner
worksheets.description: Map out your revenue streams, pricing, and projected income.

sections.heading: Revenue Streams
prompts: How will you charge (commission, fee-only, hybrid, retainer)?
What is your average revenue per client?
How many clients do you project in Year 1? Year 2? Year 3?

sections.heading: Cost Structure
prompts: Variable costs (per client or per transaction)

worksheets.usage: Complete during or after class to feed into FEC Block 4 (Capture Value)

worksheets.title: Resources & Partners Map
worksheets.description: Identify what you need to deliver value consistently.

sections.heading: Key Partners
prompts: Who helps you deliver value? (wholesalers, product providers)
Who helps you reach customers? (referral partners, centers of influence)
What value do you provide to them?
What value do they provide to you?

worksheets.usage: Complete before filling FEC Block 5 (Enable Value)

worksheets.title: UVP Differentiation Canvas
worksheets.description: Articulate what makes your advisory practice unique.

sections.heading: Competitive Positioning
prompts: How do you fill those gaps?

sections.heading: UVP Statement
sections.template: I help [target customer] [solve problem] by [unique approach], unlike [competitors] who [alternative approach].
sections.example: I help young professionals build financial independence by integrating life planning with investment advice, unlike traditional advisors who focus only on portfolio management.

worksheets.usage: Complete before finalizing FEC Block 6 (Unified Venture Proposition)

facilitatorNote: These worksheets scaffold the canvas wizard experience. Rookies can complete them on paper, in a shared doc, or directly in the canvas wizard interface.
